content:
Predictive modeling is something I want to incorporate throughout the entire work as well. I want to figure out systems and processes to have the work be different every time it's encountered, like generative, possibly having just on the textual level words change, and then on the audio level, obviously we could work with generative music, and on the visual level, generative visuals.

The paradise lost thing is also about how the fallen angels in chaos, in their absence from god, created the arts and philosophy and mathematics and Olympic Games to entertain themselves.

The language: emojis being almost hieroglyphic like, letters and sounds replaced by symbols, like Japanese and Chinese symbols, the combination of English as it evolved from old English to modern English, making you > u, or the e sounds in really into rele

The audio I want to be inspired by Brian Eno and David Lynch. Brian Eno had work that would change and evolve throughout the course of a day, or throughout the course of a week, or throughout the course of the exhibit, maybe dependent on temperature or time of day, adding elements that create chaos or unknowing or just constant change, which is similar and representative of everything in the universe on an atomic level being constantly in motion, and that also speaks to astrology, so we should incorporate that. Lynch then used the sound of audios, recordings of actors speaking the words backwards, and then would reverse it to make it sound otherworldly. We could make the speech of characters from the recordings, constant recordings being done by machines of humans speaking through time.
context: From document: OK, can you provide me a detailed summary of all the ideas we went… — chunk 9 (chunk 9/18)
